reasoning: 4,4'-methylenebis(2-chloroaniline) is an aromatic amine with two aniline rings each bearing a chloro substituent and connected by a methylene bridge; the primary structural features are chloro-substituted benzene rings bearing amine groups. While it is both a chlorobenzene and a primary aromatic amine, the direct parent capturing the halogen substitution on the aromatic ring is chlorobenzenes.
